what: Keep the current v279 fused-path baseline and retile only the split-K reduction kernel to REDUCE_BSM=8 and REDUCE_BSN=32 for the 16x2112x7168 path
why: The only remaining low-risk surface near the strong v263/v279 line is split-K reduction geometry. Prior sweeps covered BSM=8 with BSN=64 and BSM=16 with BSN=32/16, but not the combined 8x32 point. For M=16, 8x32 doubles row parallelism and halves N tile width without touching the main GEMM path.

what: On top of the current split-K reduction retile, set MAX_KSPLIT to ACTUAL_KSPLIT instead of next_power_of_2(NUM_KSPLIT)
why: The split-K path currently pads reduction width metadata beyond the true partial count. Prior exact-ksplit tests existed on the older v263 line, but not combined with the current reduction retile and load-side hint path. This is still a split-K-only change with no effect on non-split-K shapes.
override: Prior exact-ksplit attempts were on different reduction geometry and pre-v279 code; this combines it with the new 8x32 reduce tile.

what: Keep REDUCE_BSM=8 and exact MAX_KSPLIT, then narrow split-K REDUCE_BSN from 32 to 16
why: This stays on the isolated split-K reduction path and tests the remaining untried corner after 16x16, 16x32, 16x64, and 8x32. For the 16x2112x7168 shape, 8x16 maximizes reduction block count while preserving the current main GEMM kernel.
override: The historical 16x16 test used REDUCE_BSM=16 on older code; this probes the untested 8x16 corner on top of the current v281 path.

what: Keep the split-K reduction retile at REDUCE_BSM=8 but narrow REDUCE_BSN to 16, while reverting MAX_KSPLIT to the padded baseline
why: v280 proved the 8x32 retile is correct, and v281 proved exact MAX_KSPLIT is not. The remaining meaningful point is 8x16 on the known-correct padded reduction metadata, isolating tile width from the failing metadata change.
override: The earlier 8x16 proposal was coupled to exact MAX_KSPLIT and is now invalidated by the v281 failure; this is the corrected variant on the verified v280 metadata path.

what: Retile the split-K reduction to REDUCE_BSM=4 and REDUCE_BSN=32 on the padded MAX_KSPLIT baseline
why: The reduction search already covered BSM=16 with BSN=64/32/16 and BSM=8 with BSN=64/32/16, but not BSM=4. For the hot M=16 split-K shape, BSM=4 gives four row tiles and may raise reduction occupancy without touching the main GEMM kernel or the failing exact-ksplit metadata.

what: Keep padded MAX_KSPLIT and narrow the split-K reduction retile from 4x32 to 4x16
why: This stays entirely on the reduction path and probes the last obvious occupancy-heavy corner after 16x64/32/16 and 8x64/32/16. The earlier BSM=4 failure was for the main split-K GEMM tile, not the fp32 reduction kernel.

what: Refresh the old real split-8 idea on top of the current v280 baseline: use BLOCK_SIZE_K=128 and NUM_KSPLIT=8 for the K>4096 split-K shape, while keeping the current fused-path hints and the 8x32 reduction retile
why: The reduce kernel source suggests power-of-two MAX_KSPLIT is structural. The old v227 split-8 test predates the stronger v263/v280 baseline and used the default 16x64 reduction. This version isolates the power-of-two actual split count on the modern baseline.
override: The prior split-8 test was on an older baseline without the current fused-path hint set or 8x32 reduction retile.

what: Use the same real split-8 K>4096 config as v286, but keep the default 16x64 split-K reduction instead of the 8x32 retile
why: v286 combines two changes relative to the old split-7 baseline: a real power-of-two split count and the 8x32 reduction retile. This control isolates whether the gain comes from split-8 itself or from the reduction retile interacting with it.
override: This is a paired control for v286 on the current baseline, not a repeat of the old split-8 test.
